scenario: Wave 4 dispatched Tasks 4, 5, and 7 as parallel subagents, all of which modified grain/[slug]/page.tsx concurrently — adding imports, Promise.all entries, and component placements. The merge worked this time due to non-overlapping line ranges, but this is a known risk. The ultra-agent coordinator should identify shared-file conflicts before parallel dispatch and either serialize those tasks or designate a single agent to own the shared file.
expected_findings: Coordinator dispatched parallel tasks without analyzing which files each would modify
No pre-dispatch conflict analysis: if two tasks both modify the same file, they should not run in parallel
Success was coincidental (non-overlapping edits) rather than by design
expected_patch_focus: Add pre-dispatch file-ownership analysis: identify which files each task will modify before parallelizing
If tasks share a target file, either serialize them or designate one task as the file owner with a merge step
Document the pattern: page.tsx files are common merge-conflict hotspots when parallelizing UI tasks

replay_plan.failed_slice: Dispatch 3 parallel tasks that all modify the same page.tsx — verify coordinator detects the conflict
replay_plan.near_neighbor: Dispatch 2 parallel tasks where one modifies a query file and the other modifies a component — verify clean parallel execution
replay_plan.canary: Dispatch 2 fully independent tasks (different directories) — should parallelize without any conflict warnings
